mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held persistently above 4% signals restrictive monetary policy that compresses valuation multiples via elevated discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as FEDFUNDS remains elevated relative to its 12-month trend.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or high-rate periods do not show statistically lower mean returns than low-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window (a Sahm-rule-style trigger), it signals an emergent labor market downturn that historically precedes recessionary equity drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over trailing 3 months relative to its 12-month trailing minimum
failure_condition: Following trigger activation, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC conditional on trigger is not significantly different from unconditional Rank IC (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in PAYEMS growth combined with a still-elevated FEDFUNDS rate indicates the Fed is maintaining restrictive policy into a weakening labor market, amplifying recession risk beyond what either series predicts alone and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with labor slowdown
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods show forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from periods where only one condition holds (interaction term coefficient not significant at p < 0.10 in regression), or joint signal underperforms single-factor FEDFUNDS-only model in walk-forward IC.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a sustained decline in HOUST (housing starts) signals interest-rate-sensitive demand destruction that leads broader economic activity with a lag, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter specifically when FEDFUNDS is above its 2-year median.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1256.0 thousand units as of t-2mo
failure_condition: HOUST decline fails to precede negative SPX 60d returns in a tightening-cycle regime with Rank IC < 0.03, or the effect reverses sign when tested against the easing-cycle subsample used as placebo.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets incrementally price in improving real economic activity.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and forward 5-day returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, indicating no exploitable short-horizon signal.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 3%, markets price in expectations of continued restrictive Fed policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month until inflation decelerates back toward target.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3%, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, invalidating the negative-direction claim.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals resilient consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; the effect requires both conditions jointly since either series alone is a noisy demand proxy.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND UNRATE flat or declining over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 715417.0 million as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods do not outperform single-factor RSAFS-only signal in walk-forward Rank IC by a statistically significant margin, or interaction coefficient in regression is not significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below its trailing 4-quarter average signals fading economic momentum that equity markets underprice, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as growth disappointments materialize in subsequent earnings revisions.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23512.717 billion as of t-6mo
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth deceleration fails to precede negative SPX 60d returns with Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or the relationship is not distinguishable from a random walk baseline in out-of-sample tests.
